_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
procedure_name: Bleeding Ulcer - Partial Gastrectomy without Vagotomy
clinical_indications: Failure of endoscopic therapy for peptic ulcer hemorrhage
Hemodynamic instability despite vigorous resuscitation (more than a three unit transfusion)
For bleeding gastric ulcer, partial gastrectomy with Billroth I or II reconstruction is generally indicated due to malignancy risk

clinical_thresholds.note: with hemodynamic instability despite vigorous resuscitation

preauth.label: Clinical notes explaining endoscopy has failed, or other indication for proceeding with surgery

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes with detailed history, especially previous endoscopy failure, signs & symptoms, planned line of treatment, and indication for procedure?
Upper GI Endoscopy confirms the diagnosis?
Was the clinical presentation and imaging indicative of surgery?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?

common_queries: Clinical notes missing documentation of previous endoscopy failure or other clear indication for surgery.
Upper GI Endoscopy report or photographs not confirming the bleeding peptic ulcer diagnosis.
Planned line of treatment document not submitted or incomplete.
Clinical presentation or imaging not clearly indicative of surgical intervention.
Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) missing daily vitals or comprehensive treatment details.
Operative notes missing or lacking sufficient detail on the surgical steps.

clinical_key_pointers: Peptic ulcer disease (PUD) is ulceration of the gastric or duodenal mucosa due to acid and pepsin.
Complications of PUD include upper GI bleed, perforation, and gastric outlet obstruction.
Upper gastrointestinal (UGI) bleeding secondary to peptic ulcer disease is a common medical condition.

Symptoms of bleeding from peptic ulcers: hematemesis (red blood or coffee-ground emesis), melena (black, tarry stool). In rare cases, massive bleeding with hematochezia (red or maroon blood in the stool) and orthostatic hypotension.

Associated symptoms: sharp gnawing epigastric pain, worsened with gastric ulcer or relieved with duodenal ulcer by food intake. Nocturnal pain commonly awakens patient at midnight. Relapses and remissions are typical.
surgical_options: For bleeding duodenal ulcer: control of the bleeding site is the first priority. If endoscopy fails to identify the source, the proximal duodenum should be opened longitudinally for inspection of the bleeding vessel.
For bleeding gastric ulcer: partial gastrectomy with Billroth I or II reconstruction is generally indicated due to malignancy risk.
For patients with medical comorbidities and bleeding gastric ulcer: ulcer excision combined with truncal vagotomy and pyloroplasty is an option.
